title: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT
optimized_headline: Discover How ChatGPT's New Workspace Agents Transform Productivity
raw_excerpt: Workspace agents in ChatGPT are Codex-powered agents that automate complex workflows, run in the cloud, and help teams scale work across tools securely.
raw_body: Workspace agents in ChatGPT are Codex-powered agents that automate complex workflows, run in the cloud, and help teams scale work across tools securely.
speedrun: ChatGPT has introduced workspace agents powered by Codex, designed to automate complex workflows and enhance team productivity. These agents operate in the cloud, allowing seamless integration across various tools while maintaining security. This development could significantly streamline collaboration and efficiency for teams, making it easier to manage tasks and projects. As remote work continues to rise, these agents may become essential for organizations looking to optimize their operations.
why_it_matters: Teams can automate tedious tasks, saving time and reducing errors in their workflows.
This shift toward automation reflects a broader trend in the market, where efficiency and scalability are increasingly prioritized.

lenses.eli12: Workspace agents in ChatGPT act like helpful assistants that take care of complicated tasks for teams. Imagine having a robot that handles all the busywork while you focus on the important stuff. This matters because it can make everyone's job easier and more efficient, especially as remote work becomes more common.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, these workspace agents could address user needs for efficiency and streamlined processes. By automating repetitive tasks, teams can save on labor costs and focus on strategic initiatives. This could lead to faster project completion and improved team morale.
lenses.engineer: The workspace agents leverage Codex to automate workflows, which could enhance productivity by handling tasks that require coding or data management. Running in the cloud allows for scalability and secure access across multiple tools. This integration could help teams work more efficiently, but engineers should consider potential limitations in customization.
